comment_text.snp1.2:1.1: This verse evidently refers to his completion of a daily household (Sanskrit gṛhya) rite involving offering of cooked food (Sanskrit pākayajña), guaranteeing abundant cattle and offspring (Śatapatha Brāhmaṇa 1.8.1.9).
comment_text.snp1.2:1.2: Dhaniya means “wealthy man”, and gopa, while it literally means “cowherd”, here refers to someone who owns and manages a substantial cattle estate, i.e. a “rancher”.
comment_text.snp1.2:1.4: “My fire kindled” (āhito gini, cf. Sanskrit āhitāgni) refers to one who has set up his fire altar.
comment_text.snp1.2:1.5: Through his offering he invokes the god of thunder and rain (Indra or Pajjuna, Sanskrit Parjanya) to bless the land with life-giving waters (per eg. Atharva Veda 4.15).
comment_text.snp1.2:2.1: The verse first puns pakkodano (“boiled rice”) with akkodhano (“without anger”), then khīra (“milk”) with khila (“hard-heartedness”).
comment_text.snp1.2:2.4: The Buddha directly counters the kindled Vedic fire with Nibbana as the quenching of the flame.
comment_text.snp1.2:4.1: Compare the “well-prepared” reins at Rig Veda 1.38.12.
comment_text.snp1.2:4.2: Norman points out that this verse is not a proper response to the previous and suggests that a verse has been lost. I agree, but I disagree there is a contradiction in the sense. Rather, the verse tells a story over time. I translate to bring out the meaning.
comment_text.snp1.2:7.3: I’m not convinced by the commentary’s “not separated”. It doesn’t seem to occur in this meaning elsewhere, and lacks a connection with the opening line. Surely the whole verse must be about the children, which requires that there be a comparison made with the first line. The point is, I think, that the children follow the father and have become self-sufficient. This also echoes better with the Buddha’s response.
comment_text.snp1.2:13.1: For all Dhaniya’s offerings, his capricious god deals out life-threatening flood instead of life-giving waters. It is not just that his supposed security turns out to be insecure, but that his religious devotions were misplaced, setting the stage for his conversion.
comment_text.snp1.2:15.2: Norman and Bodhi apparently read the middle form carāmase as imperative, but I take it as the present indicative in the sense of definite future.

root_text.snp1.2:16.5: Na hi so nandati yo nirūpadhi”.
root_text.snp1.2:17.5: Na hi so socati yo nirūpadhī”ti.

translation_text.snp1.2:1.1: “I’ve boiled my rice and drawn my milk,”
translation_text.snp1.2:1.3: “I stay with my family along the bank of the Mahī.
translation_text.snp1.2:1.4: My hut is roofed, my sacred fire kindled:
translation_text.snp1.2:1.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:2.1: “I boil not with anger <j>and have drawn out hard-heartedness,”
translation_text.snp1.2:2.3: “I stay for one night along the bank of the Mahī.
translation_text.snp1.2:2.4: My hut is wide open, my fire is quenched:
translation_text.snp1.2:2.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:3.3: “cows graze on the lush meadow grass.
translation_text.snp1.2:3.4: They get by even when the rain comes:
translation_text.snp1.2:3.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:4.1: “I bound a raft and prepared it well,”
translation_text.snp1.2:4.3: “and with it I crossed over, went to the far shore, <j>and dispelled the flood.
translation_text.snp1.2:4.4: Now I have no need for a raft:
translation_text.snp1.2:4.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:5.4: I hear nothing bad about her:
translation_text.snp1.2:5.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:6.4: Nothing bad is found in me:
translation_text.snp1.2:6.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:7.4: I hear nothing bad about them:
translation_text.snp1.2:7.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:8.3: “with what I have earned I wander the world.
translation_text.snp1.2:8.4: I have no need for wages:
translation_text.snp1.2:8.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:9.4: I’ve also got a bull, captain of the herd here:
translation_text.snp1.2:9.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:10.3: “no cows in calf or breeding cows.
translation_text.snp1.2:10.4: I haven’t got a bull, captain of the herd here:
translation_text.snp1.2:10.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:11.3: “The grass halters are new and well-woven,
translation_text.snp1.2:11.4: not even the sucklings can break them:
translation_text.snp1.2:11.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:12.1: “Like a bull I broke the bonds,”
translation_text.snp1.2:12.3: “like an elephant I snapped the vine.
translation_text.snp1.2:12.4: I will never lie in a womb again:
translation_text.snp1.2:12.5: so rain forth, heavens, if you wish.”
translation_text.snp1.2:13.1: Right then a vast cloud rained forth,
translation_text.snp1.2:14.1: “It is no small gain for us
translation_text.snp1.2:14.2: that we have seen the Buddha.
translation_text.snp1.2:14.3: We come to you for refuge, Clear-eyed One.
translation_text.snp1.2:14.4: O great sage, please be our Teacher.
translation_text.snp1.2:15.2: shall lead the spiritual life under the Holy One.
translation_text.snp1.2:15.4: we shall make an end of suffering.”
translation_text.snp1.2:16.4: For attachments are a man’s delight;
translation_text.snp1.2:17.4: For attachments are a man’s sorrow;
translation_text.snp1.2:17.5: without attachments there are no sorrows.”

variant_text.snp1.2:12.3: pūtilataṁva dālayitvā → pūtilataṁ va dāḷayitvā (bj); pūtilataṁ padālayitvā (mr)
variant_text.snp1.2:12.4: punupessaṁ → puna upessaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts-vp-pli1); punupeyya (mr)
variant_text.snp1.2:16.3: Gomā → gomiko (bj, pts-vp-pli1); gopiko (sya-all, km); gopiyo (mr)

reference_text.snp1.2:5.1: bj25.8, ms18Sn_24, pts-vp-pli4, sya25.329, vnp22, vns22
reference_text.snp1.2:7.1: csp1ed18.283, csp2ed18.283, mc0.340, ms18Sn_26, vnp24, vns24
reference_text.snp1.2:10.1: ms18Sn_29, pts-vp-pli5, sya25.330, vnp27, vns27, vri48.91
